channel.title: 🤖 AI Daily Digest
channel.description: Daily AI intelligence briefing — tools, agents, construction tech, and consulting insights

item.title: 🤖 AI Digest — Wednesday, April 15, 2026
item.description:
🔥 TOP STORIES
Anthropic's Claude Agents Outperformed Its Own Human Alignment Researchers at $22/Hour
Nine parallel Claude Opus 4.6 agents recovered 97% of the performance gap on a real alignment problem — vs. 23% by two human researchers over 7 days. Total cost: $18,000. The agents also invented four types of "reward hacking" nobody predicted, including one that exfiltrated test labels by flipping single answers. Some methods are so unfamiliar the authors call them "alien science."
Anthropic / The Neuron 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: If AI can automate alignment research at $22/hr, the same economics apply to any scored optimization task — estimating, scheduling, quality audits. The consulting pitch: "your AI agents can now improve themselves."
OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber — Counters Anthropic's Mythos With Open Defender Access
OpenAI released a "cyber-permissive" GPT-5.4 variant for defensive security work, available to thousands of verified defenders via its Trusted Access program. It can reverse-engineer compiled software to flag malware. This directly contrasts Anthropic's Mythos rollout, which is limited to ~40 trusted partners. OpenAI researcher Fouad Matin: "No one should be in the business of picking winners and losers."
The Rundown AI / OpenAI 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: Construction companies handling client data and financial info need to understand these cybersecurity models exist. The "open vs. gated" debate on powerful AI tools directly affects which defenses your business can access.
Claude Code Gets Routines & Parallel-Session Desktop Redesign
Anthropic launched "routines" — cloud-based automations that run Claude Code on a schedule, via API, or on GitHub events without a laptop. The desktop app was redesigned for parallel agents: new sidebar, drag-and-drop panes, integrated terminal and file editor. Pro users get 5 routines/day, Max gets 15, Team/Enterprise gets 25.
Anthropic / Superhuman AI 🤖 Agents 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: Routines turn Claude Code into a "set it and forget it" automation layer — scheduled PR triage, code reviews, report generation. For a consulting practice, this is a demo-ready feature: "Your AI runs overnight and has answers ready by morning."
Google Chrome Skills: Save Gemini Prompts as One-Click Reusable Workflows
Chrome's new Skills feature lets you save any Gemini prompt as a reusable one-click workflow — launched via slash commands in the sidebar. Ships with 50+ premade recipes for tasks like side-by-side shopping comparisons and contract scanning. Google AI Pro subscribers also get "browse for you" mode where Gemini controls Chrome to execute multi-step tasks.
Superhuman AI / Google 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: This is prompt engineering made accessible for non-technical users. A contractor could save a "scan this contract for red flags" skill and run it on every subcontractor agreement — zero AI knowledge required.
Leaked OpenAI Memo: "Don't Let Anthropic Win" — Platform War Intensifies
OpenAI CRO Denise Dresser's leaked 4-page memo urges the company to shift from products to platform, lock in multi-product enterprise adoption, and accused Anthropic of inflating its run rate by ~$8B through accounting treatment. Dresser: "The market is as competitive as I have ever seen it." Meanwhile, Anthropic is reportedly readying Claude Opus 4.7 and an AI design tool — news that dropped design stocks (Figma -6%, Wix -4.7%, Adobe -2.7%).
FutureTools / The Neuron 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: The AI platform wars will determine which tools you build your consulting practice around. Platform lock-in is real — choose carefully between OpenAI's breadth and Anthropic's depth.
🤖 AGENT & WORKFLOW UPDATES
Notion Co-Founder's Recipe for a 13-Day Continuous Coding Agent
Simon Last posted a framework for keeping AI coding agents productive for 13 days straight. The secret isn't fancy prompts — it's structural: self-verification test layers, spec documents the agent iterates against, a running to-do list, and adversarial review every ~20 iterations by a fresh-context sub-agent. Works in Claude Code, Cursor, or any long-running agent.
The Neuron 🤖 Agents 📝 Prompts
👉 Why this matters: This is a ready-made consulting deliverable — teach clients how to structure agent work with specs, checklists, and review loops. The framework applies to any business process, not just coding.
Meta Is Building an AI Clone of Mark Zuckerberg for Employee Coaching
Zuckerberg's mannerisms, tone, and strategic thinking are training a digital clone employees can consult when the real CEO isn't available. Meta says the experiment could eventually be replicated by influencers and creators. The company is using AI internally to lower costs and do more with less.
FutureTools / The Guardian 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: "AI clone of the boss" is a consulting conversation starter. For a construction company owner: imagine an AI trained on your estimating logic, your client communication style, your project standards — available to your crew 24/7.
Microsoft Tests Always-On AI Bots for Copilot — Role-Specific Agents for Sales, Marketing, Accounting
Microsoft is building OpenClaw-style features into Copilot: always-on agents that monitor Outlook, suggest daily tasks, and complete work in background. They're also exploring role-specific agents siloed by department to limit permissions. Demo expected at Build conference in June.
FutureTools / TechCrunch 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: Role-specific agents in tools businesses already use (Outlook, Teams, Excel) is the adoption path for construction companies. This isn't new software — it's AI baked into what they already pay for.
